## Test Scenarios
- /api/health returns 200 and minimal payload
- /api/health/ready returns 200 or 503 with boolean checks
- /api/health/status returns 403 for non-admin

## Files to Create
- tests/integration/health/basic.test.ts
- tests/integration/health/ready.test.ts
- tests/integration/health/status.test.ts

## Success Criteria
- Health endpoints are stable
- Responses are consistent and safe

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-41l0","title":"Fix focusTrap visibility check for fixed-position elements","description":"getFocusableElements filtered by offsetParent, which is null for position: fixed; focus traps in fixed dialogs (quick actions, modals) could see zero focusable elements. Use getClientRects + computed style to detect visibility.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-12T16:39:24.990680893-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T16:39:54.559165381-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-12T16:39:54.559165381-05:00","close_reason":"Replace offsetParent visibility check with getClientRects + computed style so fixed-position focusables are included."}
+{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-45d4","title":"Audit Unit Tests for Mocks & Fakes","description":"## Objective
Perform a comprehensive audit of all existing unit tests (`*.test.tsx`, `*.test.ts`) to identify usage of mocks, stubs, spies, and fake timers.

## Scope
- Scan `apps/web/src/**/*.test.{ts,tsx}`
- Scan `packages/cli/__tests__/**/*.test.ts`
- Scan `vitest.setup.ts` and other config files

## Deliverables
- A report listing every file using `vi.mock`, `vi.spyOn`, `vi.useFakeTimers`, or manual stubs.
- Classification of each mock usage:
    - **Critical (Must Remove):** Mocks of business logic or core libraries.
    - **Platform (Maybe Allow):** Mocks of browser APIs not present in JSDOM (e.g. `ResizeObserver`, `matchMedia`).
    - **Acceptable:** Mocks of external services (though prefer E2E for these).

## Criteria for Success
- A clear list of 'Mock Violations' is produced.
- A prioritized list of tests to be refactored is created as subtasks.

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-4dx","title":"Implement theme provider and dark mode toggle","description":"**Details:**
- ThemeProvider wrapping app for dark/light mode
- Persists preference to localStorage
- Respects system preference on first visit
- Dark mode toggle in Nav component
- CSS variables switch based on theme

**Implementation:**
```typescript
// apps/web/src/components/theme-provider.tsx
'use client';
import { createContext, useContext, useEffect, useState } from 'react';

type Theme = 'light' | 'dark' | 'system';

export function ThemeProvider({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {
  const [theme, setTheme] = useState<Theme>('system');
  
  useEffect(() => {
    const stored = localStorage.getItem('theme') as Theme;
    if (stored) setTheme(stored);
  }, []);
  
  useEffect(() => {
    const root = document.documentElement;
    const isDark = theme === 'dark' || 
      (theme === 'system' && window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)').matches);
    root.classList.toggle('dark', isDark);
    localStorage.setItem('theme', theme);
  }, [theme]);
  
  return (
    <ThemeContext.Provider value={{ theme, setTheme }}>
      {children}
    </ThemeContext.Provider>
  );
}
```

**Toggle Component:**
- Sun/Moon icon button
- Cycles through light → dark → system
- Tooltip showing current mode

**Location:** 
- apps/web/src/components/theme-provider.tsx
- apps/web/src/components/theme-toggle.tsx

**Reasoning:** Dark mode is expected in developer tools. System preference respect shows attention to detail. Persistent preference provides consistent UX.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:34:00.527000897-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T02:08:43.738433267-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T02:08:43.738433267-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented ThemeProvider with light/dark/system modes, ThemeToggle component, integrated into Providers.tsx. Ready for Nav component to include the toggle. Build and tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-4dx","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-vsk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:37:03.111792364-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-4o18","title":"Build Swap Meet browse and discovery UI","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-msj.

## Task
Build the Swap Meet (community prompts) browse and discovery UI.

## Pages

### /swap-meet (Community Browse)
Main community prompts gallery page.

Components:
- Hero section with "Swap Meet" branding and search
- Category pills for quick filtering
- Sort dropdown (Trending, Newest, Top Rated)
- Grid of PromptCard components
- Infinite scroll pagination
- "Featured" section for editor picks
- Empty state when no results

### /swap-meet/[id] (Prompt Detail)
Individual prompt view page.

Components:
- Full prompt content with syntax highlighting
- Copy to clipboard button
- Save to library button
- Fork button
- Author info with avatar and reputation
- Rating display and rate buttons
- Tags list
- Related prompts section
- Share button

## Shared Components

### PromptCard
Reusable card for displaying prompts in grids.

Props:
- prompt: PromptData
- showAuthor: boolean
- showRating: boolean
- onSave: () => void
- onRate: (value) => void

Features:
- Title and truncated description
- Category badge
- Rating indicator (approval %)
- Save/bookmark icon
- Quick copy button
- Hover preview

### CategoryFilter
Horizontal scrollable category pills.

### SortDropdown
Dropdown for sort options with icons.

### RatingButtons
Thumbs up/down buttons with counts.

## Styling
- Use existing Tailwind + Framer Motion patterns
- Card hover animations
- Smooth infinite scroll
- Mobile-responsive grid (1 col mobile, 2 tablet, 3 desktop)

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-gas","title":"Create README.md for repository","description":"**Details:**
- Comprehensive README covering all aspects of the project
- Quick start guide for users
- Installation instructions (web, CLI, skills)
- Development setup guide
- Architecture overview
- Contributing guidelines link
- About the Author section with ecosystem links

**README Structure:**
```markdown
# JeffreysPrompts.com

> Curated prompts for agentic coding. Browse, copy, install as Claude Code skills.

## Features
- 🔍 Instant search with BM25 ranking
- 📋 One-click copy to clipboard  
- 🛠️ Install as Claude Code skills
- 📦 Prompt bundles for workflows
- 🌐 Works offline (PWA)
- 🤖 CLI tool with JSON output for agents

## Quick Start

### Web
Visit [jeffreysprompts.com](https://jeffreysprompts.com)

### CLI
\`\`\`bash
curl -fsSL https://jeffreysprompts.com/install-cli.sh | bash
jfp list
\`\`\`

### Claude Code Skills
\`\`\`bash
jfp install --all
# or specific prompts
jfp install idea-wizard readme-reviser
\`\`\`

## CLI Usage
[Full command reference]

## Development
[Setup, build, test instructions]

## Architecture  
[Monorepo structure diagram]

## About the Author
Jeffrey Emanuel (@doodlestein)
- jeffreyemanuel.com
- agent-flywheel.com

## License
MIT
```

**Reasoning:** README is the entry point for the repository. - Refactor any tests using raw `console.log` or standard Playwright steps to use `logger.step`, `logger.info`, etc.

- Test reports include screenshots for failures.

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-gn9y","title":"EPIC: Admin Dashboard & Moderation","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-lbq.

## Overview
Admin dashboard and content moderation tools for platform management.

## Access Control
- Admin role in user_profiles (is_admin boolean or role field)
- Middleware to protect /admin routes
- Admin-only API endpoints

## Admin Dashboard Features

### 1. Overview Dashboard (/admin)
- Total users (and growth chart)
- Active subscriptions (MRR)
- Content counts (prompts, packs, skills)
- Recent signups
- Recent activity

### 2. User Management (/admin/users)
- Search/filter users
- View user details
- View user's content
- Impersonate user (careful!)
- Suspend/ban user
- Adjust subscription manually

### 3. Content Moderation (/admin/moderation)
- Queue of reported content
- Review and take action (approve, remove, warn)
- View reporter info
- Appeal handling

### 4. Featured Content (/admin/featured)
- Mark prompts/packs/skills as featured
- Manage "Prompt of the Day"
- Editor's picks curation

### 5. System Settings (/admin/settings)
- Feature flags
- Maintenance mode toggle
- Global announcements

## Database
- reports (content reports from users)
- admin_actions (audit log of admin actions)
- featured_content (featured items with positions)
- Add is_admin to user_profiles
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:22:23.299330089-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T01:22:23.299330089-05:00"}

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-l1t7","title":"Typography Standardization: Eliminate arbitrary text sizes","description":"# Epic: Typography Standardization

## Background & Problem Statement

The UI overhaul established a 5-size typography scale in globals.css:
- text-xs (12px)
- text-sm (14px)
- text-base (16px)
- text-lg (18px)
- text-xl+ (headings)

However, several components still use arbitrary values like \`text-[11px]\` and \`text-[10px]\`, which:
1. Break the design system's rhythm
2. Are harder to maintain
3. Create subtle inconsistencies between similar elements

## Files Affected
1. PromptDetailModal.tsx - 3 instances of text-[11px]
2. SpotlightSearch.tsx - 1 instance of text-[11px], 3 instances of text-[10px]

## The Fix
Replace all arbitrary text sizes with the nearest standard size:
- \`text-[11px]\` → \`text-xs\` (12px, +1px - negligible visual difference)
- \`text-[10px]\` → \`text-xs\` (12px, +2px - slightly larger but more readable)

## Implementation Note
**Typography fixes are included in the color fix tasks:**
- g0oe (PromptDetailModal) - includes "Also Fix Typography" section
- usn5 (SpotlightSearch) - includes "Also Fix Typography" section

This keeps related changes together (same lines of code) and reduces PR churn.

## Design Rationale
- 10-11px text is often too small for comfortable reading
- The extra 1-2px improves accessibility without breaking layouts
- Consistency trumps pixel-perfect sizing for this use case

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Zero instances of \`text-[Npx]\` in component files
- [ ] All text uses standard Tailwind classes
- [ ] No layout shifts from size changes
- [ ] Text remains readable at all sizes

## Validation
The color validation tests (tzmc) include a typography consistency check that will verify no arbitrary text sizes remain.

## Dependencies
- Typography fixes are bundled into g0oe and usn5 tasks
- tzmc validates the result

## Estimated Scope
- 2 files (via g0oe and usn5)
- 7 class replacements
- Minimal testing required","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-12T00:37:31.250583614-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T17:54:54.515685149-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-12T17:54:54.515685149-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed all text-[Npx] patterns: Nav.tsx and BottomTabBar.tsx now use text-xs. Zero arbitrary text sizes remain in component files."}
+{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-l2ji","title":"Refactor Unit Tests to Integration Style","description":"## Objective
Refactor unit tests identified in the audit to remove unnecessary mocks and move towards integration-style testing where possible.

## Scope
- Refactor critical violations identified in the audit.
- For UI components, prefer testing real interactions over mocked props/hooks.
- For CLI commands, verify real outputs rather than spying on console.log where possible (using capture methods).

## Constraints
- Do NOT remove platform mocks (e.g. `window.matchMedia`) if they are required for the test environment to function.
- Do NOT rewrite the entire test suite; focus on high-value refactors.

## Criteria for Success
- Reduced usage of `vi.mock` for internal modules.
- Tests still pass with high reliability.

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-l35x","title":"E2E Tests: Admin Dashboard & Moderation","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-whb.

## Task
Create comprehensive E2E tests for the admin dashboard with detailed logging.

## Test Scenarios

### Admin Access Tests
1. **Access Control**
   - Non-admin user cannot access /admin
   - Admin user can access /admin
   - Admin role is verified on each request
   - Tampering with role fails

### User Management Tests
2. **User List**
   - List all users with pagination
   - Search users by email/name
   - Filter by tier (free/premium)
   - Filter by status (active/suspended/deleted)
   - Sort by join date, name, etc.

3. **User Detail**
   - View user profile details
   - View subscription history
   - View content (prompts, packs, skills)
   - View activity log

4. **User Actions**
   - Suspend user account
   - Unsuspend user account
   - Grant premium manually
   - Revoke premium
   - Delete user (soft delete)
   - Impersonate user (admin debug)

### Content Moderation Tests
5. **Reported Content**
   - View reported content queue
   - See report details and reason
   - View the reported content
   - Approve content (dismiss reports)
   - Remove content from Swap Meet
   - Warn content creator
   - Ban repeat offenders

6. **Featured Content**
   - Mark prompt as featured
   - Set "Prompt of the Day"
   - Manage featured carousel
   - Schedule featured content

### Analytics Tests
7. **Dashboard Metrics**
   - Total users (by tier)
   - New signups (daily/weekly/monthly)
   - Active users
   - Subscription revenue (MRR)
   - Churn rate
   - Content metrics (prompts, packs, skills)

8. **Export Data**
   - Export user list to CSV
   - Export revenue reports
   - Export content reports

### System Management Tests
9. **Announcements**
   - Create system announcement
   - Set announcement visibility
   - Schedule announcement
   - Dismiss announcement

10. **Maintenance Mode**
    - Enable maintenance mode
    - Users see maintenance page
    - Admins can still access
    - Disable maintenance mode

## Logging Requirements
- Log all admin actions with admin ID
- Log before/after state for user changes
- Log moderation decisions with reason
- Log impersonation sessions
- Screenshot admin dashboard states
- Log analytics query performance

## Test Infrastructure
- Create test admin user
- Create test regular users to manage
- Seed reported content for moderation
- Clean up test data after suite

## Files to Create
- `e2e/admin/access.spec.ts`
- `e2e/admin/user-management.spec.ts`
- `e2e/admin/content-moderation.spec.ts`
- `e2e/admin/analytics.spec.ts`
- `e2e/admin/system.spec.ts`
- `e2e/lib/admin-helpers.ts`

## Success Criteria
- Admin access properly restricted
- User management flows work
- Moderation actions apply correctly
- Analytics display accurate data
- Audit trail is complete

Expand E2E test coverage to include critical user flows that are currently missing or under-tested.

## Critical Flows to Cover
1. **Full Installation Flow:**
   - Simulate user copying install command.
   - Verify proper generation of `curl | bash` scripts (mocking the server response if needed for stability, or running against local server).
2. **Search & Filter:**
   - Test complex filter combinations (Category + Tag + Search).
   - Verify semantic vs BM25 search toggle results (mocking the search endpoint to guarantee deterministic results for E2E).
3. **Theme Toggling:**
   - Verify light/dark mode persistence across reloads.
4. **Mobile Responsiveness:**
   - Verify bottom sheet interactions on mobile viewports.
   - Verify touch gestures (swipe to basket) - *Note: This might be hard to test reliably in Playwright, investigate options*.

## Criteria for Success
- New spec files added for the above flows.
- All new tests use `PlaywrightLogger` for detailed reporting.

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-l87c","title":"EPIC: Content Reporting & Moderation","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-5ly.

## Overview
Content reporting and moderation workflow for community safety.

## Report Flow (User Side)

### 1. Report Button
- Available on all public content (prompts, packs, skills)
- Icon button (flag) with "Report" tooltip
- Only for logged-in users
- Can't report own content

### 2. Report Dialog
- Reason selection:
  - Spam or misleading
  - Inappropriate content
  - Copyright violation
  - Other (with text field)
- Optional additional details
- Submit button

### 3. Confirmation
- "Report submitted" toast
- "We'll review this within 24 hours"

## Moderation Flow (Admin Side)

### 1. Moderation Queue
- List of pending reports
- Sort by: date, report count, severity
- Filter by: status, type, reason

### 2. Review Interface
- View reported content
- View reporter info and reason
- View report history for this content
- Actions:
  - Dismiss report (no action)
  - Warn author
  - Remove content
  - Ban user

### 3. Actions
- Remove: Set visibility to 'removed', notify author
- Warn: Send warning email, record strike
- Ban: Suspend account, notify user

## Database Schema
```typescript
export const contentReports = pgTable("content_reports", {
  id: uuid("id").primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  reporterId: uuid("reporter_id").notNull(),
  contentType: text("content_type").notNull(),
  contentId: uuid("content_id").notNull(),
  reason: text("reason").notNull(),
  details: text("details"),
  status: text("status").default("pending"),  // pending, reviewed, actioned, dismissed
  reviewedBy: uuid("reviewed_by"),
  reviewedAt: timestamp("reviewed_at"),
  action: text("action"),  // none, warned, removed, banned
  createdAt: timestamp("created_at").defaultNow(),
});
```

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-wi4g","title":"Add removable filter chips showing active filters","description":"# Task: Add Active Filter Chips with Remove Buttons

## Problem Statement
When users apply filters, the only indication is:
1. The pill button is highlighted (easy to miss if scrolled)
2. Text below results: "15 prompts for 'testing' with tags: unit-tests, automation"

Users can't:
- See at a glance what filters are active
- Remove individual filters without scrolling back
- Quickly understand why results are limited

## Current Behavior
```
Results Header
├── "Testing" (h2, if category selected)
├── "15 prompts" (p, count)
└── "with tags: unit-tests, automation" (p, inline text)
```

## Proposed Solution
Add a row of removable chips between filter controls and results:

```tsx
// ActiveFilterChips.tsx
interface ActiveFilterChipsProps {
  query: string;
  category: PromptCategory | null;
  tags: string[];
  onRemoveQuery: () => void;
  onRemoveCategory: () => void;
  onRemoveTag: (tag: string) => void;
  onClearAll: () => void;
}

export function ActiveFilterChips({
  query,
  category,
  tags,
  onRemoveQuery,
  onRemoveCategory,
  onRemoveTag,
  onClearAll,
}: ActiveFilterChipsProps) {
  const hasFilters = query || category || tags.length > 0;
  
  if (!hasFilters) return null;
  
  return (
    <div className="flex flex-wrap items-center gap-2 py-4 border-b border-neutral-200 dark:border-neutral-800">
      <span className="text-sm text-neutral-500 dark:text-neutral-400 mr-1">
        Active filters:
      </span>
      
      {/* Search query chip */}
      {query && (
        <FilterChip
          label={`"${query}"`}
          icon={<Search className="w-3 h-3" />}
          onRemove={onRemoveQuery}
        />
      )}
      
      {/* Category chip */}
      {category && (
        <FilterChip
          label={category}
          icon={<Folder className="w-3 h-3" />}
          onRemove={onRemoveCategory}
          className="capitalize"
        />
      )}
      
      {/* Tag chips */}
      {tags.map((tag) => (
        <FilterChip
          key={tag}
          label={tag}
          icon={<Tag className="w-3 h-3" />}
          onRemove={() => onRemoveTag(tag)}
        />
      ))}
      
      {/* Clear all */}
      <button
        onClick={onClearAll}
        className="ml-2 text-xs text-neutral-500 hover:text-neutral-900 dark:hover:text-white transition-colors"
      >
        Clear all
      </button>
    </div>
  );
}

// Individual chip component
function FilterChip({ 
  label, 
  icon, 
  onRemove,
  className 
}: { 
  label: string; 
  icon?: React.ReactNode;
  onRemove: () => void;
  className?: string;
}) {
  return (
    <span
      className={cn(
        "inline-flex items-center gap-1.5 px-2.5 py-1 rounded-full",
        "bg-neutral-100 dark:bg-neutral-800 text-sm text-neutral-700 dark:text-neutral-300",
        "group transition-colors hover:bg-neutral-200 dark:hover:bg-neutral-700",
        className
      )}
    >
      {icon && <span className="text-neutral-400 dark:text-neutral-500">{icon}</span>}
      <span className="max-w-[150px] truncate">{label}</span>
      <button
        onClick={onRemove}
        className="ml-0.5 p-0.5 rounded-full text-neutral-400 hover:text-neutral-600 dark:hover:text-neutral-200 hover:bg-neutral-300 dark:hover:bg-neutral-600 transition-colors"
        aria-label={`Remove ${label} filter`}
      >
        <X className="w-3 h-3" />
      </button>
    </span>
  );
}
```

## Integration in page.tsx

```tsx
{/* Filter Controls */}
<div className="mb-4 space-y-4">
  <CategoryFilter ... />
  <TagFilter ... />
</div>

{/* Active Filter Chips */}
<ActiveFilterChips
  query={filters.query}
  category={filters.category}
  tags={filters.tags}
  onRemoveQuery={() => setQuery("")}
  onRemoveCategory={() => setCategory(null)}
  onRemoveTag={(tag) => setTags(filters.tags.filter(t => t !== tag))}
  onClearAll={clearFilters}
/>

{/* Results */}
<div className="mt-6">
  <h2>...</h2>
  <PromptGrid ... />
</div>
```

## Design Details
- **Chip style**: Rounded pill with subtle background
- **Icon per type**: Search icon for query, folder for category, tag for tags
- **Remove button**: X on right side, larger hit target on hover
- **Truncation**: Long values truncate with ellipsis
- **"Clear all"**: Text link at end, not a chip

## Accessibility
- Each chip's X button has aria-label: "Remove {filter} filter"
- Chips are focusable via keyboard
- Screen reader: "Active filters: search 'testing', category debugging, tag unit-tests"

## Animation (Optional)
- Chips animate in/out when added/removed
- Use Framer Motion AnimatePresence

## Testing
1. Apply search query → chip appears with query text
2. Apply category → chip appears with category name
3. Apply multiple tags → multiple tag chips appear
4. Click X on chip → that filter removed, results update
5. Click "Clear all" → all filters removed
6. Keyboard: Tab through chips, Enter to remove
7. Screen reader: chips announced correctly

## Files to Create/Modify
- NEW: apps/web/src/components/ActiveFilterChips.tsx
- MODIFY: apps/web/src/app/page.tsx

## Dependencies
- Should be done after "Clear All Filters" button redesign
- Uses same filter state from useFilterState hook","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T00:48:11.636820907-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T10:08:08.95787059-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-12T10:08:08.95787059-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented ActiveFilterChips component with search/category/tag chips, X buttons for removal, icons per type, proper accessibility (aria-labels, keyboard navigation), and data-testid for E2E testing. Integrated in page.tsx between filters and results. Build passes, component passes color consistency tests.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-wi4g","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9rgl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T01:26:05.845097741-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-wlg3","title":"CLI: Add collections command for premium users","description":"## Task
Add a collections command to the jfp CLI for premium users to browse and use their collections.

## Commands
- jfp collections - List user's collections
- jfp collections <name> - Show prompts in collection
- jfp collections <name> --export - Export collection to files
- jfp collections create <name> - Create new collection (syncs to premium)
- jfp collections add <collection> <prompt-id> - Add prompt to collection

## Integration Points
- Premium API: /api/cli/collections (from premium project)
- Requires authentication

## Output Formats
- Default: Pretty table with prompt counts
- --json: JSON output for scripting
- --export: SKILL.md files or markdown

## Success Criteria
- List and browse collections
- Export collections locally
- Create and modify collections (syncs to cloud)
- Works with synced local cache

## Cross-Repo Integration
- Premium CLI collections endpoints (via CLI sync API): jeffreysprompts_premium-67o","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T00:30:54.686074336-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T12:01:23.736549191-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-12T12:01:23.736549191-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented collections create/add/export commands, prompt resolution, and help updates","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-wlg3","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-uw7z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T00:31:41.066778838-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-wlg3","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-hiff","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T00:31:46.195827853-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

@@ -0,0 +1,38 @@
+# Test Suite Audit: Mocks & Fakes
+
+## Summary
+A comprehensive scan of the codebase reveals widespread usage of mocks. While some are necessary for JSDOM environments (platform mocks), others mock core business logic or external libraries, which reduces confidence in the actual integration.
+
+## 1. Critical Violations (Prioritize Refactoring)

+| `apps/web/src/components/mobile/SwipeablePromptCard.test.tsx` | `useHaptic`, `useIsMobile`, `use-basket`, `useSwipeHint`, `toast` | **Heavy Mocking**. Delete this unit test and rely on Mobile E2E tests. |

+
+## 2. Platform Mocks (Acceptable in Unit Tests)

+| `vitest.setup.ts` | `framer-motion` | JSDOM lacks layout engine for animations. |
+
+## 3. Standard Test Utilities (Acceptable)
+
+- `vi.fn()` for event handler props (e.g., `onClick={vi.fn()}`)

+- `vi.spyOn(console, ...)` for noise suppression
